document.title: Cybersecurity Risk Management Report
standards: Cybersecurity in Medical Devices: Quality System Considerations and Content of Premarket Submissions

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity risk management report for H&abyz G1417/G1717 series digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by documenting cybersecurity risk analysis, STRIDE threat identification, CVSS-based assessment, mitigation, residual risk evaluation, and post-production monitoring.
keyPoints: The report was prepared before commercial distribution of the G1417/G1717 detector models.
A total of 17 cybersecurity risks were identified through STRIDE threat modeling.
Initial evaluation found 6 acceptable risks and 11 unacceptable risks.
After mitigation, all 17 residual risks were evaluated in the broadly acceptable region.
Cybersecurity risks are to be reviewed once a year, with updates when risks, legal requirements, or product changes occur.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: G1417/G1717 Series
entities.evidence: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors that is used for screening and diagnosis of disease of injury.

entities.name: RA
entities.evidence: RA 1. Monitoring the national and international standards

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk management
concepts.evidence: preparation of cyber security risk management system in accordance with ISO 14971:2019, TR 24971:2020, AAMI TIR 57

concepts.name: STRIDE threat modeling
concepts.evidence: A total of 17 risks were identified through STRIDE Threat modeling.

concepts.name: CVSS assessment
concepts.evidence: the cyber threats were scored according to CVSS

concepts.name: Residual risk acceptability
concepts.evidence: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in a broadly acceptable region.

concepts.name: Production and post-production monitoring
concepts.evidence: Collection and review activities in production and post-production phase

concepts.name: Secure communication controls
concepts.evidence: Subsequent communication is encrypted (AES) with the symmetric key.

evidence.claim: The document is revision 0 dated December 05, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Cybersecurity Risk Management Report Rev. Date December 05, 2024

evidence.claim: The report covers eight detector models: G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The intended use is diagnostic radiographic exposure of body parts by qualified or trained doctors or technicians.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are intended for use by a qualified/trained doctor or technician on both adult subjects for taking diagnostic radiographic exposures of the skull, spinal column, chest, abdomen, extremities, and other body parts.

evidence.claim: The detectors are not intended for mammography applications.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.

evidence.claim: The product software is AXD version 1.0.0.
The report references ISO 14971, IEC 62304, AAMI TIR 57, NIST SP 800-30, and FDA cybersecurity premarket submission guidance among other standards and references.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 14971 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices; IEC 62304 Medical device software - Software life-cycle processes; AAMI TIR 57 Principles for medical device security; NIST Special Publication 800 30 Guide for Conducting Risk Assessments; Cybersecurity in Medical Devices: Quality System Considerations and Content of Premarket Submissions

evidence.claim: A total of 17 cybersecurity risks were identified through STRIDE threat modeling.
evidence.evidenceText: A total of 17 risks were identified through STRIDE Threat modeling. There are 17 risks distributed across Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information disclosure, Denial of service, and Elevation of privilege.

evidence.claim: Initial cybersecurity risk assessment used likelihood, impact, risk level, and CVSS score.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk ID Likelihood (Before) Impact (Before) Risk Level (Before) CVSS Score

evidence.claim: Initial risk evaluation found 6 acceptable risks and 11 unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Green zone, Acceptable Risk 6 Red zone, Unacceptable Risk 11 Total 17

evidence.claim: After mitigation, the residual risk was reduced to 9 very low risks and 8 low risks.
evidence.evidenceText: After mitigation measures were taken for a total of 17 risks (Very Low: 2 / Low: 4 / Medium: 10 / High: 1), the residual risk was reduced to a total of Very Low: 9 / Low: 8.

evidence.claim: All identified residual risks were evaluated in the broadly acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in a broadly acceptable region.

evidence.claim: The report states no additional risk control measures are needed after residual risk evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: So, there are no further additional risk control measures needed.

evidence.claim: Communication mitigation includes certificate verification, asymmetric key exchange, and AES encryption.
evidence.evidenceText: During the connection establishment procedure, a certificate and asymmetric key are received, and after verification with the signature of the certificate, a symmetric key encrypted with the received asymmetric key is delivered. Subsequent communication is encrypted (AES) with the symmetric key.

evidence.claim: Post-production cybersecurity activity includes annual cybersecurity risk review and updates when threats are identified.
evidence.evidenceText: Review your cybersecurity risks once a year and update them if you believe there is a threat to your cybersecurity.

evidence.claim: Customer cybersecurity issues are handled through change management and service processes.
evidence.evidenceText: In the event of a cyber security issue from a customer, communicate with the customer according to the change management procedure and service process.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal is provided in source metadata as MFDS/domestic, but the document text does not explicitly name MFDS or a regulatory authority.
ProductSignal metadata is Unclassified, while the document text identifies the product as Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and lists model names.
OCR/text quality contains minor spacing and wording artifacts such as 'metho ds', 'Treat modeling', and 'waning', but key metadata and risk tables remain readable.
The source is marked confidential and intended solely for H&abyz Co., Ltd.; wiki reuse may require access-control review.
